key_points: Limited student feedback on dorms; McGill mentioned as a single option
Transfer students report feeling isolated and disconnected from residential life
No strong praise or complaints about housing quality in available data
key_quotes: What is McGill like? Living there in single, also a sophomore transfer.
It's been about two months since school started, and honestly, I've been having a really hard time connecting with people. I'm usually just in my room doing homework.

key_points: Greek life dominates social scene; non-members feel excluded
Transfer students repeatedly report inability to make friends
Finance clubs are ultra-competitive and reportedly rude to newcomers
key_quotes: I didn't realize how big of a deal greek life really was... it all came to an abrupt halt when I suffered the bad luck of [not getting in].
I have 0 idea what I'm doing wrong people seem to can't stand me, and they only hit me up when they need help on assignments.
Some of the people running them have been unbelievably rude to me for no reason. And the multi-round interview for student orgs is ridiculous.

key_points: Almost no direct student feedback on dining quality in available data
Vanderbilt mentioned positively in a 'perfect school' post for its 'charm' but not food
Nashville food scene praised as a compensating factor off-campus
key_quotes: My parents will be in town for a week (they eat everything), was wondering what your favorite bites to eat were in Nashville?
Rand cookie heist — Circa Nov 2021

key_points: Multiple students report depression, isolation, and loneliness at Vandy
One student scraped by with 2.0 first semester due to depression
Lonely students post publicly asking for things to do and friend-making tips
key_quotes: I've been kind of in a pretty sad slump and i dont rlly have any friend atm and ill be pretty lonely during fall break.
My first semester was terrible, I was depressed as shit and ended up scraping by with a 2.0 GPA.
I see so many posts saying they are miserable at Vandy. My daughter is thinking of applying ED and those kinds of things make us very concerned.

key_points: Financial aid can be excellent — full rides and Chancellor's Scholarships exist
Aid reportedly dropped mid-enrollment for one student with no warning; office ignored them
Total cost of attendance hit $101,380/year for 2026-27 with quiet fee increases
key_quotes: My friend who had been committed for a month and declined all their offers suddenly got their fin aid increased by $30k so now they can't pay, and the fin aid office is basically ignoring them.
Tuition is ridiculous (2026-2027): $101,380. How could they raise fees quietly like that without publicly announcing it?
For such a supposedly snobby school, their finaid is REALLY good.

key_points: Coursework is genuinely harder than high school; students from strong backgrounds still struggle
Grade inflation is real — many report easy 3.5+ GPAs, especially in non-STEM fields
Premed track is tough; harder to get good grades than at comparable schools
key_quotes: Here, if I know all the material, create a study guide, take practice tests, do constant flash cards... I still get a 70.
Grade inflation is real. I was able to easily get a 3.5. I have seen so many people with 3.8.
Vandy cons: harder to get good grades in premed reqs.

key_points: Peabody College used ChatGPT to write a condolence email after a mass shooting
Class registration info withheld from incoming students until very late
Financial aid office described as unresponsive and dismissive by multiple students
key_quotes: Peabody College at Vanderbilt University apologized for using ChatGPT to write a sensitive email... The message asked students to 'come together' following a shooting at Michigan State.
Class registration is like next week and they haven't given us any information about how or what classes to register for.
The admissions officers are almost impossible to get ahold of, they are incredibly rude when you do get to talk with them.

key_points: Nashville food and music scene is a genuine asset students appreciate
California and Northeast students report culture shock adjusting to the South
Campus described as charming; Parthenon at sunset mentioned as a highlight
key_quotes: I'm from California and the culture here is quite different imo. I'm Asian American so the vibes in California vs Tennessee are very different.
Nashville has tons of opportunities — really like the idea of being there.
Vanderbilt has the most aura.

key_points: Finance and consulting recruiting is active but clubs are brutally competitive
Multi-round interviews for student organizations feel gatekeeping and elitist
Nashville location provides hospital and healthcare access for premeds
key_quotes: The finance clubs are ultra-competitive, but some of the people running them have been unbelievably rude to me for no reason. And the multi-round interview for student orgs is ridiculous.
Career-wise I was surprised how unenjoyable it is.
Nashville has tons of opportunities.

key_points: At $101K/year sticker price, value depends entirely on merit aid or financial aid
Full rides and Chancellor's Scholarships make it a steal for some students
Without significant aid, cost is hard to justify vs. peer schools
key_quotes: Total cost of attendance is now $101,380/year. How could they raise fees quietly like that?
Vandy would be little to nothing a year... Vandy is far superior to UF, and costs you less!! What a gift!
Parents, how do you afford Vandy and is it worth it? The thought of 95k per year is absolutely dumbfounding.

key_points: Reputation is 'preppy party school meets serious academics' — both stereotypes are real
Athletics culture has surged recently with football success creating genuine school pride
Socially stratified by Greek life; outsiders feel the gap acutely
key_quotes: People quite literally crying because they 'only got into Vanderbilt.'
Is Vanderbilt 'nerdy' or 'cool'? I definitely hear everything from 'smart kid nerdy school' to 'oh vandy is a party school.'
Inside Vanderbilt: The 'new Harvard' where young people actually have fun.

key_points: Race: Black student reports people crossing the street and avoiding eye contact with him on campus
Administration: ChatGPT used for mass shooting condolence email; no one reviewed it before sending
Social exclusion: Multiple transfer students report total social isolation months after arriving
key_quotes: Everytime I walk by someone or get up close to someone they either put they head down, cross the street or pretend to be on their phone — I promise you guys I'm not going to rob you.
So when you are a Dean, you apologize for a ChatGPT essay. But as an applicant, that same Dean will disqualify me if I send an AI generated one.
I've been having a really hard time connecting with people. I'm usually just in my room doing homework... I tried reaching out to a couple people to hang out, but no luck.

key_points: Surprise acceptance packages mailed to your door — no portal update needed
Chancellor's Scholarship is a structured, well-funded merit program worth pursuing
Former AO confirmed Vanderbilt genuinely values quirky, memorable extracurriculars
key_quotes: My mom called my name and said there was a package at the door... turns out it was an invitation to attend MOSAIC and early acceptance! And I found out I got the Chancellor's Scholarship!
There are ECs that are impressive and there are ECs that are memorable – and they aren't always the same. Hell yeah, I want to see [the death metal ukulele transposition].
I got off the waitlist for Vanderbilt with a close full ride!!!! MY DREAM SCHOOL!
